title: Daily Market Digest: March 29, 2026
subtitle: Market closes week with significant losses amid geopolitical tensions

items.headline: U.S. Markets Cap Fifth Straight Losing Week as Iran War Concerns Persist
items.summary: U.S. equities concluded their fifth consecutive week of declines on Friday, March 27, 2026, marking the longest losing streak in nearly four years. Investor sentiment remained overwhelmingly bearish amidst escalating geopolitical tensions related to the Iran war, which continues to drive oil prices higher and fuel fears of a global recession. All major indices suffered significant losses for the week, with the Dow Jones Industrial Average entering correction territory. Consumer sentiment also dropped to its lowest level since December 2025. This report is for informational purposes only and does not constitute financial advice.

items.statContext: Down 1.7% for the day, 8.7% below January highs.
Sank 2.15% for the day, entered correction territory.
Dropped 1.73% for the day, entered correction territory.

items.headline: Tilly's, Inc. Leads Monthly Gainers
items.summary: Tilly's, Inc. ($TLYS) was the best-performing stock in March 2026, with a substantial gain.

items.headline: Sable Offshore Corp. Rises Significantly
items.summary: Sable Offshore Corp. ($SOC) showed strong performance over the month of March.

items.headline: Marex Group Surges on Q1 Update
items.summary: Marex Group ($MRX) was a top mid-cap gainer following a positive Q1 trading update, expecting revenue between $667M-$697M.

items.headline: Estée Lauder Leads Weekly Declines
items.summary: Estée Lauder Companies ($EL) was the worst-performing stock of the week.

items.headline: Coinbase Global Falls Amid Crypto Sell-off
items.summary: Coinbase Global ($COIN) experienced a significant price decline as crypto stocks faced a broad sell-off.

items.headline: Amazon Shares Dip
items.summary: Amazon ($AMZN) was among the big tech stocks weighing on the market, as consumers potentially cut back on discretionary spending.

items.headline: Iran War Drives Oil Prices Above $100, Threatens Global Economy
items.summary: The ongoing conflict with Iran has pushed Brent crude oil prices past $100 a barrel, reaching $114 on Friday, March 27, 2026. This surge, coupled with the effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz, represents the 'largest supply disruption in the history of the global oil market,' intensifying fears of global recession and stagflation.

items.headline: Federal Reserve Holds Rates Steady, Inflation Expectations Rise
items.summary: The Federal Reserve maintained its benchmark interest rate at 3.5%-3.75% during its March 18 meeting. Despite this, inflation expectations for the year increased from 3.4% to 3.8% in March, the largest one-month rise since April 2025, suggesting persistent inflationary pressures.

items.headline: Defensive Sectors Lead Amid Risk-Off Sentiment, Tech and Discretionary Lag
items.summary: A clear 'risk-off' rotation is evident, with defensive sectors like Energy (up 6.12% for the week), Utilities, Consumer Staples, and Basic Materials (up 4.23% for the week) showing strength. In contrast, Technology (down 3.51% for the week) and Consumer Discretionary (the worst-performing sector this week) are lagging significantly, reflecting investor caution.

items.headline: Mixed Earnings Reports Reflect Economic Headwinds
items.summary: While some companies like Carnival Corporation ($CCL), Cintas ($CTAS), Paychex ($PAYX), and Delek US Holdings ($DK) reported stronger-than-expected Q1 or Q3 fiscal 2026 earnings, management outlooks are becoming more cautious due to rising costs and economic uncertainty.

items.headline: Consumer Sentiment Plunges to Multi-Month Low
items.summary: The University of Michigan's consumer sentiment index dropped 6% in March to 53.3, its lowest level since December 2025. This decline, particularly sharp among middle to higher-income consumers, indicates growing pessimism about the economic fallout from the Iran war and rising gasoline prices.
